reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly encodes all parts of the informal statement. The condition $n \ge 1$ becomes `P.natDegree ≥ 1`; since $a_0 = \pm 1 \ne 0$ the leading coefficient is nonzero so natDegree equals $n$. The requirement "$a_i = \pm 1$ for all $0 \le i \le n$" is captured by `∀ k ∈ Set.Icc 0 P.natDegree, P.coeff k = 1 ∨ P.coeff k = -1` — since the $k$-th coefficient of $\sum a_i x^{n-i}$ is $a_{n-k}$, the range of indices matches. The roots-are-real condition uses `∀ z : ℂ, P.eval z = 0 → ∃ r : ℝ, r = z`, which (via the standard `Coe ℝ ℂ` = `Complex.ofReal` instance) becomes `↑r = z`, equivalent to `z.im = 0`. The set equality asks to characterize all such polynomials, matching "find all". There is no drift in hypotheses, conclusion, types, or quantifier structure.
